name: cubicweb-jsonschema
documentation:
```{image} https://jenkins.logilab.org/job/cubicweb-jsonschema/badge/icon
:target: https://jenkins.logilab.org/job/cubicweb-jsonschema/
```

# cubicweb-jsonschema

[cubicweb-jsonschema] provides mappings from CubicWeb entities to [JSON Schema]
documents as well as an HTTP API based on the [JSON Hyper-Schema]
specification.

Documentation is available at <https://cubicweb-jsonschema.readthedocs.io/>

## Testing

Tests can be run using:

```
python -m unittest discover -s test
```

from top-level directory after having installed test dependencies from file
`test-requirements.txt`.

Some tests make use of the [ajv] program (more specifically [ajv-cli]) to
validate JSON Schema response against meta schema. This can be installed using
`npm` and should be in `$PATH` when running tests. If not available
validation would not occur.

[ajv]: http://epoberezkin.github.io/ajv/
[ajv-cli]: https://github.com/jessedc/ajv-cli
[cubicweb-jsonschema]:  https://www.cubicweb.org/project/cubicweb-jsonschema
[json hyper-schema]:  http://json-schema.org/latest/json-schema-hypermedia.html
[json schema]: http://json-schema.org/

classes.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.views.JSONSchemaSectionRelationTags

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.views.JSONSchemaSectionRelationTags.relations_by_section
documentation:
return a list of (rtype, role, target types) for the given entity
matching 'section' and 'permission'.

JSON Schema configuration relation tag.

This relation tag can be used to configure generation of JSON Schema
documents through uicfg. It accepts the following sections:

*   'inlined': relation definition would appear as an inlined document of
    the main entity's JSON Schema,
*   'hidden': relation definition is hidden from JSON Schema, and,
*   'related': relation definition would not appear in entity's JSON
    Schema but would appear in a rel="related" hypermedia link.

functions.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.api.entities.cubicweb_requesterror
documentation:
Handler for CubicWeb's RequestError instances.

It will return a RFC 7807 formatted error response from exception found in
context.

This view breaks backward-compatibility with CubicWeb's behaviour without
cubicweb-jsonschema as the JSON response returned by CubicWeb is not
application/problem+json.

functions.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.api.entities.generic_error
documentation:
Generic exception handler for exception (usually during "edition"
operations).

It will return a RFC 7807 formatted error response from exception found in
context.

modules.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.testing
documentation: doctest utilities

functions.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.testing._parse_webtest_testresponse
documentation:
Parse a webtest.TestResponse string into a (status, headers, body)
tuple.

```pycon
>>> from webtest import TestResponse
>>> r = TestResponse(b'{a: 1}', status=200,
...                  headerlist=[('Content-Type', 'application/json')])
>>> _parse_webtest_testresponse(str(r))
('Response: 200 OK', [('Content-Type', 'application/json')], '{a: 1}')
>>> r = TestResponse(b'hello!', status=200,
...                  headerlist=[('this', 'that'),
...                              ('Host', 'example.fr')])
```

```pycon
>>> _parse_webtest_testresponse(str(r))
('Response: 200 OK', [('Host', 'example.fr'), ('This', 'that')], 'hello!')
>>> r = TestResponse(status=204)
>>> _parse_webtest_testresponse(str(r))
('Response: 204 No Content', [], '')
>>> r = TestResponse(status=204, headerlist=[('Host', 'example.fr')])
>>> _parse_webtest_testresponse(str(r))
('Response: 204 No Content', [('Host', 'example.fr')], '')
>>> _parse_webtest_testresponse(
...     'Response: 204 No Content<BLANKLINE><BLANKLINE>')
('Response: 204 No Content', [], '')
>>> _parse_webtest_testresponse('ah')  # doctest: +IGNORE_EXCEPTION_DETAIL
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
ParseError: not a webtest.TestResponse string
```

functions.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.testing.jsondiff_filter_ellipsis
cubicweb_jsonschema.testing._headerlist_to_dict
documentation:
Convert a list of headers tuples into a dict of lists.

```pycon
>>> _headerlist_to_dict([('Location', 'www.example.com')])
{'Location': ['www.example.com']}
>>> headers = _headerlist_to_dict([('Location', 'there'), ('Foo', 'bar'),
...                                ('Foo', 'baz')])
>>> list(sorted(headers.items()))
[('Foo', ['bar', 'baz']), ('Location', ['there'])]
```

functions.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.testing._headerlist_to_string
documentation:
Convert a list of headers tuples into a multiline string.

```pycon
>>> _headerlist_to_string([('Location', 'www.example.com')])
'Location: www.example.com'
>>> print(_headerlist_to_string([('Location', 'there'),
...                              ('Content-Type', 'application/json')]))
Location: there
Content-Type: application/json
```

functions.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.links.serialize_links
documentation:
Serialize link_dict into a Link Header
compliant string as defined by RFC 5988

functions.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.links._split_unquotted
documentation:
Split a string using using 'delimiter' as separator
when it does not split a quoted string:

```pycon
>>> _split_unquotted(',', 'a,"b,c",d')
['a', '"b,c"', 'd']
```

A quoted string can contain an escaped quote:

```pycon
>>> _split_unquotted(',', r'"a","\"b,\""')
['"a"', '"\\"b,\\""']
```

functions.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.links.parse_links
documentation: Parse a link header and generate a MultiDict This function generates a MultiDict of links by parsing a 'Link' header's value: ```pycon >>> from pprint import pprint >>> link_header = ( ... ';rel="a";type="application/json",' ... ';rel="b";type="text/xml",' ... ';rel="b";type="text/xml"' ... ) >>> links = parse_links(link_header) >>> pprint(links.getone('a')) {'href': '/A', 'type': 'application/json'} >>> pprint(links.getall('b')) [{'href': 'B1', 'type': 'text/xml'}, {'href': 'B2', 'type': 'text/xml'}] ``` This implementation allows spaces between links and between parameters: ```pycon >>> links = parse_links('; rel="a" , ;rel="b" ; type="text/plain"') >>> pprint(links.dict_of_lists()) {'a': [{'href': '/A'}], 'b': [{'href': '/B', 'type': 'text/plain'}]} ``` It's possible to quote parameter values... ```pycon >>> links = parse_links('; rel="foo,bar"') >>> pprint(links.dict_of_lists()) {'foo,bar': [{'href': '/X'}]} ``` ...and to escape quotes in quoted values: ```pycon >>> links = parse_links('; title="The \"quotation\"" ; rel="related"') >>> pprint(links.dict_of_lists()) {'related': [{'href': '/X', 'title': 'The "quotation"'}]} ``` If a parameter is present and has no value specified, its default value will be True: ```pycon >>> links = parse_links('; use_modal; rel="next"') >>> pprint(links.dict_of_lists()) {'next': [{'href': '/X', 'use_modal': True}]} ``` Link values with several relations can also be parsed: ```pycon >>> links = parse_links('; rel="a b c"') >>> pprint(links.dict_of_lists()) {'a': [{'href': '/X'}], 'b': [{'href': '/X'}], 'c': [{'href': '/X'}]} ```

documentation:
Decorator for resource class's __init__ method to bind the __parent__
attribute to instance.

documentation:
Return (`rtype`, `role`) if `etype` has a relationship `rtype` else
raise a ValueError.

functions.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.resources.need_cnx
documentation:
Method decorator checking if the request is bound to an authenticated
user (for which request.cw_cnx is not None).

Metaclass for pluggable resources, ensuring they have their own
_child_resources dictionary.

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.resources.PluggableResource.child_resource
documentation:
Register `child_resource`, that should be a class inheriting from
{class}`TraversalResource`.

It may be used as a class decorator.

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.resources.PluggableResource.matching_resource
documentation:
Return the resource matching `value` child segment or None if no one
matches.

If there are more than on matching resource, raises `ValueError`.

Base class for pluggable resources, which provides an API to register
custom child resources.

% automethod: child_resource

classes.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.resources.TraversalResource

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.resources.TraversalResource.match
documentation:
Take the parent resource's instance as argument and returning `True`
if this resource applies to this context or `False` otherwise.

Abstract base class for custom child resource. Concrete classes should
specify both `segment` class attribute and `match` method.

Add "title" and "description" metadata to `schema` if found on
`mapper`.

functions.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.base.add_links
documentation:
Method decorator that collects Hyper-Schema links and possibly add them
to JSON Schema produced by `schema_and_definitions` method.

classes.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.base.ProtectedDict
documentation: A dict which keys cannot be overridden.

classes.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.base.JSONSchemaMapper

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.base.JSONSchemaMapper.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a JSON Schema mapper from a CubicWeb `_cw` and a Pyramid
`resource`.

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.base.JSONSchemaMapper.select_mapper
documentation:
Return a mapper with `regid` selected with `**ctx`.

The `resource` bound to this mapper is passed in selection context,
unless specified in `**ctx`.

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.base.JSONSchemaMapper.json_schema
documentation:
Return the JSON Schema for bound Yams context.

If `schema_role` is not None a plain JSON Schema document is returned
following specified *role* ("view", "creation" or "edition").
Otherwise, a JSON Hyper Schema document with *links* is returned.

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.base.JSONSchemaMapper.schema_and_definitions
documentation:
Return the JSON (sub-)Schema for bound Yams context along with a
dict of subschemas to be inserted in the main JSON Schema's
"definitions" property.

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.base.JSONSchemaMapper.links
documentation:
Yield Link appobjects matching regid and selection context of this
mapper.

Extra keyword arguments are used for Link appobjects selection (in
addition to mapper's section context).

Abstract base class for mappers between Yams schema definitions and
JSON Schema documents.

classes.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.base.JSONSchemaDeserializer

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.base.JSONSchemaDeserializer.values
documentation:
Return a dict holding deserialized data from `instance` suitable for
insertion in CubicWeb database for mapped schema element.

Items from `instance` dictionnary are pop-ed upon processing.

Abstract mixin class for mappers capable of deserializing a JSON
`instance` valid under mapped JSON Schema and insert respective data into
CubicWeb's database.

classes.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.base.JSONSchemaSerializer

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.base.JSONSchemaSerializer.serialize
documentation: Return the serialized value for mapped schema element.

Abstract mixin class for mappers capable of serializing data from
CubicWeb's database as a JSON `instance` valid under mapped JSON Schema.

Yield Link appobjects matching regid and selection context of this
mapper if schema_role is None.

Base abstract class to fill the gap between a yams relation and it's json

They should be selected depending on the relation (`etype`, `rtype`, `role`
and optionaly `target_types`).

Return a dictionary holding deserialized value for mapped attribute.

If mapped attribute is absent from `instance` and `entity` is not
None, the default value for attribute is returned as value of the
dictionnary.

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.relation.AttributeMapper._type
documentation:
Return properly typed value for use within a cubicweb's entity from
given JSON value.

Nothing to do by default.

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.relation.AttributeMapper._value
documentation: Return the serializable value from attribute `value`.

Abstract base abstract class to map attribute relation.

Concrete class should implement the `json_type` property.

Return role-cardinality if schema definition is consistent and

Abstract class for true relation (as opposed to attribute) mapper.

Map relation as 'inlined', i.e. the target of the relation is

classes.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.relation.RelationMapper

Map relation as 'generic', i.e. the target of the relation may be

present but 'target_types' is optional.

Return True if relation from selection context is "inlined" per
jsonschema uicfg.

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.collection.CompositeRelationItemMapper.schema_and_definitions

In "creation" role, schema a oneOf with possible target types entity
JSON schema. Otherwise, depending on wether the relation in "inlined"

a genertic collection item JSON Schema.

documentation: Predicate that returns 1 when the supplied `rtype` is not final.

functions.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.predicates.yams_component_target
documentation:
Predicate that returns 1 when the target entity types are component of
the relation defined by `rtype` and `role` (i.e. the relation has
composite set to `role`).

functions.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.predicates._for_workflowable_entity
documentation:
Return 1 if `for_entity` context argument corresponds to a worflowable
entity type.

classes.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.predicates.yams_match
documentation:
Predicate that returns a positive value when the supplied relation
match parameters given as predicate argument.

The more __call__ arguments match __init__'s ones, the higher the score
is.

classes.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.predicates.partial_yams_match
documentation:
Return non-zero if selected class's attributes `etype`, `rtype`, `role`
and/or `target_types` match with keyword arguments from context.

classes.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.compound.CompoundMapper

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.compound.CompoundMapper.etype
documentation: entity type holding this compound field

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.compound.CompoundMapper.relations
documentation: sequence of relations gathered in this compound field

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.compound.CompoundMapper.title
documentation: title of the field

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.compound.CompoundMapper.name
documentation:
name of the property to be inserted in the main JSON Schema
document (defaults to class name).

methods.name: cubicweb_jsonschema.mappers.compound.CompoundMapper.required
documentation: Return False by default.

Mapper for a "compound" field gathering Yams relations into a
dedicated JSON "object" to be inserted in "definitions" key of the JSON
Schema document.

The compound "object" will appear in the main JSON Schema document's
properties under the `name` class attribute (defaults to class name).

documentation: An *unrelated* link that should not be selected.

methods.name: test.test_mappers.RelatedCollectionMapperTC.test_custom_mapper
documentation:
Make sure a custom mapper with a yams_match selector wins selection
over default one.

methods.name: test.test_mappers.RelationMapperTC.test_generic_relation_items_view
documentation: In "view" role we only get existing targets in the "oneOf".

methods.name: test.test_mappers.CompoundMapperTC.test_no_duplicate_relation_mapping
documentation:
A CompoundMapper with the same relation 'flash' is defined in
test/data/mappers.py, thus this one is a duplicate and should fail.

methods.name: test.test_mappers.CompoundMapperTC.test_no_duplicate_subject_relation_mapping
documentation:
A CompoundMapper with the same relation 'flash' is defined in
test/data/mappers.py, thus this one is a duplicate and should fail.

methods.name: test.test_mappers.CompoundMapperTC.test_name_unicity_constraints_per_entity_type
documentation:
Several CompoundMapper with the same name may be registered if they
concern distinct entity types.

methods.name: test.test_mappers.CompoundMapperTC.test_mapped_attributes_hidden
documentation:
Make sure attributes mapped to CompoundMapper are in "hidden" uicfg
section.

methods.name: test.test_mappers.EntityMapperTC.test_schema_nested
documentation: Check several nested inlined and compound relations.
